title: Perityle scopulorum
intro: Perityle scopulorum, also known as Desert Marigold, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, with its benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Perityle scopulorum include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Perityle scopulorum is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves for skin issues and wound healing.
taxonomy: Perityle scopulorum is classified as a species within the genus Perityle, which is part of the family Asteraceae. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Perityle, Species: P. scopulorum. Perityle scopulorum is considered an annual or perennial plant. Its classification is subject to change as new research is conducted in the field of botany.
